title: Unit Changes Ability Auto Cast State
description: This event fires when a unit changes an ability’s auto cast state.  Use“Triggering Ability”to get the ability that was used.  Use“Triggering Player”to get the player that used the ability.  Use“Triggering Unit”to get the unit that used the ability.  If the parameter“Include Shared Abilities”is set to true, instead of matching the specific event, it will match any event that has the same ability set id (AbilSetId).

title: Unit Uses Ability

Use“Triggering Ability Stage”to get the stage the ability was in when the event fired.  Use“Triggering Ability Target Point”to get the target point for the ability.  Use“Triggering Ability Target Unit”to get the target unit for the ability.  Use“Triggering Player”to get the player that used the ability.  Use“Triggering Unit”to get the unit that used the ability.  If the parameter“Include Shared Abilities”is set to true, instead of matching the specific event, it will match any event that has the same ability set id (AbilSetId).

title: Create Actor Scope
description: Creates an empty actor scope.  You can get the scope that was created by using“Last Created Actor Scope”.  An actor scope is a container for a set of actors.  Therefore, this action is only useful if you intend to create actors in the scope you create. Actor Name is an optional name for the actor to create, not the true name of the scope. If the name is “*” or “**” it is ignored, otherwise the function will try to create an actor with the specified name to put into the scope.

title: Send Actor Message To Game Region With Filters
description: Sends an actor message to all the actors contained within the specified game region.  The Intersect Type parameter defines which radius will be used to determine intersection with the region.  The Class Filters parameter is used to specify which types of actors should receive the message.  The format for Class Filters is a list of comma delimited actor types.  The Terms parameter is used to specify a condition that an actor in the region must meet in order to receive the message.  The format for Terms is a list of semi colon delimited actor terms.  An actor message will perform an action and/or trigger an event on the actor it is sent to.
grammar: Send actor message message to all actors that intersect game region region with their intersectType that belong to class classFilters and match terms terms
prototype: void libNtve_gf_SendActorMessageToGameRegionWithFilters(region lp_region,int lp_intersectType,string lp_message,string lp_classFilters,string lp_terms,);

title: Anim Baseline Start
description: Returns an AnimBaselineStart actor message.  This message can be used to tell an actor to begin playing its baseline animations, which include animations such as stand and move.  It will only work on actors with models that have animations.
grammar: AnimBaselineStart

title: Anim Blend Time Remove
description: Constructs and returns an AnimBlendTimeRemove actor message based on the parameters.  This message will remove any existing animation blend time messages on the actor it is sent to, causing it to revert to the default animation blend time.  It will only work on actors with models that have animations.
grammar: AnimBlendTimeRemove

title: Anim Blend Time Apply
description: Constructs and returns an AnimBlendTimeApply actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set the default blend time for animations played by the actor it is sent to.  It will only work on actors with models that have animations.
grammar: AnimBlendTimeApply blendTime

title: Anim Bracket Start
description: Constructs and returns an AnimBracketStart actor message based on the parameters.  This message tells an actor to begin an anim bracket with the specified parameters.  An anim bracket is refered to by its name, determined by the name parameter, and consists of three animations defined as Opening Props, Content Props, and Closing Props.  The Opening Props parameter determines which animation the actor will play at the beginning of the anim bracket.  The Content Props parameter determines the intermediate animation.  If Content Props is set to an animation that loops it will play until the anim bracket is ended, otherwise it will only play once.  The Closing Props parameter determines which animation is played when the anim bracket ends.  It will only work on actors with models that have animations.
grammar: AnimBracketStart animName openingProps contentProps closingProps animBracketStartFlags timeVariant timeType
prototype: native string MakeMsgAnimBracketStart(string animName,string propsOpening,string propsContent,string propsClosing,int animBracketStartFlags,fixed timeVariant,int timeType,);

title: Anim Bracket Resume
description: Constructs and returns an AnimBracketResume actor message based on the parameters.  This message will resume a stopped animation bracket being executed by an actor.  It will only work on actors with models that have animations.
grammar: AnimBracketResume animName animTransitionFlags timeVariant timeType
prototype: native string MakeMsgAnimBracketResume(string animName,int animTransitionFlags,fixed timeVariant,int timeType,);

title: Anim Bracket Stop
description: Constructs and returns an AnimBracketStop actor message based on the parameters.  This message will stop an anim bracket with the same name as the Anim Name parameter being played by an actor, causing it to play its closing animation and return to normal behavior.  It will only work on actors with models that have animations.
grammar: AnimBracketStop animName animTransitionFlags timeVariant timeType

grammar: Create actor content

title: Attach Set Position
description: Constructs and returns an AttachSetPosition message.  Sets the position of a model’s attach point (or potentially multiple attach points if you use an attach method) to the specified world coordinates.
grammar: AttachSetPosition attachMethods position

title: Destroy
description: Constructs and returns a Destroy actor message.  This message destroys the actor it is sent to, removing it from the game entirely.
grammar: Destroy

title: HostSiteOpsSet
description: Constructs and returns a HostSiteOpsSet actor message.  This is used to change the site ops for an actor.  The Host Name parameter is used to indicate which host you want to modify the site ops for.  Typically, this will just be ::Host, but could refer to other host references as well.
grammar: HostSiteOpsSet hostName ops holdPosition holdRotation
prototype: string libNtve_gf_HostSiteOpsSet(string lp_hostName,string lp_ops,int lp_holdPosition,int lp_holdRotation,);

title: Death Customize
description: Constructs and returns a DeathCustomize actor message.  Used for alternate death sequences depending on different conditions.  Example: burrowed/unburrowed
grammar: DeathCustomize subname

title: List Add
description: Constructs and returns a ListAdd actor message.  This can only be sent to an actor list.  Adds the referenced actor to the actor list.  The purpose of a list is so all actors in the list could have the same actions applied (example: tree doodads in an area all catch fire).
grammar: ListAdd actorRefName

title: Missile Tentacle Return
description: Constructs and returns a MissileTentacleReturn actor message.  This message causes an actor to return to its origin point immediately.  It will only work on missile actors that are flagged as tentacles.
grammar: MissileTentacleReturn

title: Model Swap
description: Constructs and returns a ModelSwap actor message.  This message will swap the current model of an actor with an model to the specified Model and Variation number.
grammar: ModelSwap model variation

title: Model Event Suppress
description: Constructs and returns a ModelEventSuppress message.  Suppress a class of model events.  This allows you to toggle whether a specific model event affects an actor.
grammar: ModelEventSuppress value event

title: List Remove
description: Constructs and returns a ListRemove actor message.  This can only be sent to an actor list.  Removes the referenced actor from the actor list.
grammar: ListRemove actorRefName

title: Mover Set Destination Z
description: Constructs and returns a MoverSetDestinationZ actor message.  This message set an actor’s absolute height based on the parameter set.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverSetDestinationZ value

title: Mover Stop
description: Constructs and returns a MoverStop actor message.  Tells the mover to stop, it will decelerate based on its current deceleration value before coming to a complete halt.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverStop

title: Mover Set Destination From
description: Constructs and returns a SetMoverDestinationFrom actor message.  This message alters an actor’s 2D map grid destination relative to the actor referenced.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverSetDestinationFrom actorRefName

title: Mover Set Speed Max
description: Constructs and returns a MoverSetSpeedMax actor message.  This will set the movers maximum speed.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverSetSpeedMax value

title: Mover Set Acceleration
description: Constructs and returns a MoverSetAcceleration actor message.  This will set the acceleration velocity of the mover.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverSetAcceleration value

title: Mover Set Deceleration
description: Constructs and returns a MoverSetDeceleration actor message.  This will set the deceleration velocity of the mover.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverSetDeceleration value

title: Mover Set Destination H
description: Constructs and returns a SetMoverDestinationH actor message.  This message alters an actor’s relative height based on the ground level and the parameter set.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverSetDestinationH value

title: Mover Set Speed
description: Constructs and returns a MoverSetSpeed actor message.  This will set the movers current speed.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverSetSpeed value

title: Mover Move
description: Constructs and returns a MoverMove actor message.  Starts the actor in motion according to the conditions that have been given to the mover.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverMove

title: Mover Set Speed From Duration
description: Constructs and returns a MoverSetSpeedFromDuration actor message.  This will set the movers speed based on a duration of time.  A shorter time will cause it to move faster.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverSetSpeedFromDuration value
raw_text: Mover Set Speed From Duration—string<actormsg>— libNtve_gf_MoverSetSpeedFromDuration

title: Mover Stop Now
description: Constructs and returns a MoverStopNow actor message.  Stops the mover directly and immediately regardless of deceleration or speed.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverStopNow

title: Mover Set Destination 2D
description: Constructs and returns a SetMoverDestination2D actor message.  This message alters an actor’s 2D map grid destination based on the X,Y parameters.  It will only work on a mover site actor.
grammar: MoverSetDestination2D x y

title: Multiply Scale
description: Constructs and returns a MultiplyScale actor message.  This will multiply the current scale of the actor by the multipliers specified over the duration specified.  Will only work on actors with a model.
grammar: MultiplyScale x y z duration
prototype: string libNtve_gf_MultiplyScale(fixed lp_x,fixed lp_y,fixed lp_z,fixed lp_duration,);

title: Print
description: Constructs and returns a Print actor message.  This message will display the String parameter in the debug log.  It is a useful debugging tool for ensuring that an actor is behaving as desired, as oftentimes actors are behind the scenes.
grammar: Print string

title: Query Radius
description: Constructs and returns a QueryRadius actor message.  This message will search a radius equal to the Radius parameter around an actor, and send an actor message determined by the Response Actor parameter to any actors it finds in that radius.
grammar: QueryRadius radius responseActor

title: Query Region
description: Constructs and returns a QueryRegion actor message.  This message searches a region actor determined by the Region Actor parameter and sends a message determined by the Response Actor parameter to any actors found inside of the region actor.
grammar: QueryRegion regionActor responseActor

title: Ref Destroy
description: Constructs and returns a RefDestroy actor message based on the parameters.  This message destroys an existing actor reference with a name equal to the Actor Ref Name parameter.  Once it is destroyed it will no loner be accessible.
grammar: RefDestroy actorRefName

title: Ref Set
description: Constructs and returns a RefSet actor message based on the parameters.  This message will store an actor determined by the Ref Source parameter, into an existing actor reference determined by the Actor Ref Name parameter.  Note: This will only set a value in an actor reference that already exists, it will not create a new reference.
grammar: RefSet actorRefName refSource

title: Ref Dump
description: Constructs and returns a RefDump actor message based on the parameters.  This message will display the contents of an actor reference, chosen through the Actor Ref Name parameter, to the debug log.  This is a useful debugging tool as it allows you to see what is being stored inside of the selected actor reference.
grammar: RefDump actorRefName

title: Ref Clear
description: Constructs and returns a RefClear actor message.  The Actor Ref name parameter determines the actor ref to clear.  This will remove the actor associated with that reference, but leave the reference for future use.  An actor ref can have any type of actor assigned to it.
grammar: RefClear actorRefName

title: Query Persistent
description: Constructs and returns a QueryPersistent actor message.  This message will instruct a region actor to send a message to other actors when they enter or leave that region actor.  The Enter Response Actor parameter determines what message will be sent to actors entering the region actor, and the Leave Response Actor determines the actor message sent to actors exiting the region actor.  This message only works on region actors.
grammar: QueryPersistent enterResponseActor leaveResponeActor

title: Ref Create
description: Constructs and returns a RefCreate actor message.  This message creates an actor reference that client code and data can use to store references to other living actors.  The Actor Ref Name parameter determines the name of the actor reference, which is used to call it through other actor messages.
grammar: RefCreate actorRefName

title: Ref Notify
description: Constructs and returns a RefNotify actor message based on the parameters.  Typically used with a target, this message allows one actor to send a ref to another actor.  The signal parameter allows you to differentiate RefNotify messages if multiple messages are used in the same context.
grammar: RefNotify actorRefName subName

title: Ref Set From Actor
description: Constructs and returns a RefSetFromActor actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set an actor relative to another actor.  For example,“RefSetFromActor ::global.test ::Creator ::Host”would set the ::global.test ref to the host of the actor that created the actor from which this message is being sent.  Note: This will only set a value in an actor reference that already exists, it will not create a new reference.
grammar: RefSetFromActor actorRefName refPrimary refSecondary

title: Ref Set From Request
description: Constructs and returns a RefSetFromRequest actor message based on the parameters.  This message uses an actor request to determine what actor should be stored in the reference.  The Ref Name parameter is the reference to store the actor in once it is found.  Note: Utilizing this function requires a strong knowledge of the actor system.
grammar: RefSetFromRequest refName subject effectName requestScope requestActor
prototype: native string MakeMsgRefSetFromRequest(string refName,string subject,string effectName,int requestScope,int requestActor,);

title: Ref Set From Msg
description: Constructs and returns a RefSetFromMsg actor message based on the parameters.  This message will store an actor determined by the Message parameter, into an existing actor reference determined by the Actor Ref Name parameter.  Note: This will only set a value in an actor reference that already exists, it will not create a new reference.
grammar: RefSetFromMsg actorRefName message

title: Ref Set Refresh Name
description: Constructs and returns a RefSetRefreshName actor message based on the parameters.  This can be used to update a ref which doesnt update automatically in the actor system, although most should update automatically.  Actor should automatically refresh references in special cases (for instance, you have a squib hosted off a hydralisk which turns into an egg.  Actor needs to update the host of the squib to host off an analogous attach point on the egg), but this is there just in case it’s needed at some point.  Note: This will only set a value in an actor reference that already exists, it will not create a new reference.
grammar: RefSetRefreshName actorRefName refreshName

title: Set Facing
description: Constructs and returns a SetFacing actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set the 2D facing of an actor according to the Facing parameter.  It will only work on actors that have bearings, such as model actors or doodad actors.
grammar: SetFacing facing

title: Set Bearings
description: Constructs and returns a SetBearings actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set the 3D position and 3D rotation of an actor.  The Position X, Y, and Z parameters correspond to the 3D map grid location.  The Forward X,Y,Z parameters and the Up X,Y,Z parameters correspond to the 3D rotation of the actor.  It will only work on actors that have bearings, such as model actors or doodad actors.
grammar: SetBearings positionX positionY positionZ forwardX forwardY forwardZ upX upY upZ
prototype: string libNtve_gf_SetBearings(fixed lp_positionX,fixed lp_positionY,fixed lp_positionZ,fixed lp_forwardX,fixed lp_forwardY,fixed lp_forwardZ,fixed lp_upX,fixed lp_upY,fixed lp_upZ,);

title: Set Height
description: Constructs and returns a SetHeight actor message based on the parameters.  This message sets the actor at a height relative to the terrain, rather than specifying an absolute Z axis value.  It will only work on actors that have bearings, such as model actors or doodad actors.
grammar: SetHeight height

title: Set Position
description: Constructs and returns a SetPosition actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set the 3D position of an actor, determined by the X,Y,Z parameters.
grammar: SetPosition x y z

title: Set Bearings H
description: Constructs and returns a SetBearingsH actor message based on the parameters.  This message operates similarly to a SetBearings message but rather than specifying an absolute Z value you specify a Height value, which is relative to the game terrain level.  It will only work on actors that have bearings, such as model actors or doodad actors.
grammar: SetBearingsH positionX positionY height forwardX forwardY forwardZ upX upY upZ
prototype: string libNtve_gf_SetBearingsH(fixed lp_positionX,fixed lp_positionY,fixed lp_height,fixed lp_forwardX,fixed lp_forwardY,fixed lp_forwardZ,fixed lp_upX,fixed lp_upY,fixed lp_upZ,);

title: Set Opacity
description: Constructs and returns a SetOpacity actor message based on the parameters.  This message sets an actor*“s opacity to the Opacity parameter value.  The available range 0 to 1.  The Blend Duration parameter determines how long it should take to blend from the actor”*s current opacity to the desired opacity.  If the Blend Duration parameter is left at 0, it will change opacity instantly.
grammar: SetOpacity opacity blendDuration

title: Set Bearings From
description: Constructs and returns a SetBearingsFrom actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set the 3D position and 3D rotation of an actor to the same 3D position and 3D rotation of another actor chosen with the Actor parameter.  It will only work on actors that have bearings, such as model actors or doodad actors.
grammar: SetBearingsFrom actor

title: Ref Table Dump
description: Constructs and returns a RefTableDump actor message based on the parameters.  This message dumps an entire actor reference table.  The Space parameter determines which ref table will be dumped: 1 will dump the global ref table, 2 will dump the scope ref table, and 3 will dump the actor ref table.  When a table is dumped, all existing actor references in that table’s category will be printed to the debug log.
grammar: RefTableDump space

title: Set Position 2D H
description: Constructs and returns a SetPosition2DH actor message based on the parameters.  This message sets the 2D position of an actor, determined by the X,Y parameters, while preserving the current height value.
grammar: SetPosition2DH x y

title: Set Position 2D
description: Constructs and returns a SetPosition2D actor message based on the parameters.  This message sets the 2D position of an actor, determined by the X,Y parameters.  Note: Using this message will set the Z position of the actor to the default terrain level.
grammar: SetPosition2D x y

title: Set Position From
description: Constructs and returns a SetPositionFrom actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set the 3D position of an actor to the 3D position of another actor, specified through the Actor parameter.
grammar: SetPositionFrom actor

title: Set Physics State
description: Constructs and returns a SetPhysicsState actor message based on the parameters.  This message will dictate to the actor how its associated physics should behave.  It will only work on actors with a model.
grammar: SetPhysicsState state delayLow delayHigh
prototype: native string MakeMsgSetPhysicsState(int physicsState,fixed delayLow,fixed delayHigh,);

title: Set Position H
description: Constructs and returns a SetPositionH actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set the 2D position of an actor to the X,Y parameters, while adjusting its height relative to the terrain determined by the Height parameter.
grammar: SetPositionH x y height

title: Set Render To Texture Enabled
description: Constructs and returns a SetRenderToTextureEnabled actor message.  This will enable or disable a model’s ability to use render to texture.  Only works on actors with models that are set up for render to texture in the model file.
grammar: SetRenderToTextureEnabled enabled
raw_text: Set Render To Texture Enabled—string<actormsg>— libNtve_gf_SetRenderToTextureEnabled

title: Set Rotation From
description: Constructs and returns a SetRotationFrom actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set the 3D rotation of an actor to the 3D rotation of another actor specified through the Actor parameter.
grammar: SetRotationFrom actor

title: Sound Add DSP
description: Constructs and returns a SoundAddDSP actor message based on the parameters.  This message will add a reverb effect, chosen by the Effect parameter, to a sound.  This will only work on sound actors.
grammar: SoundAddDSP effect

title: Set Tint Color
description: Constructs and returns a SetLocalTintColor actor message based on the parameters.  This will set an actors tint color for all players.  This will only work on actor’s with a model.
grammar: Set Tint Color color with HDR hdr|Multiplier over duration seconds

title: Set Z
description: Constructs and returns a SetZ actor message based on the parameters.  This message sets the absolute height of the actor, determined by the Z parameter.
grammar: SetZ z

title: Set Local Tint Color
description: Constructs and returns a SetLocalTintColor actor message based on the parameters.  This will set an actors tint color for only the current player.  This will only work on actor’s with a model.
grammar: SetLocalTintColor color

title: Signal
description: Constructs and returns a Signal actor message based on the parameters.  This message will send a signal with a name determined by the Signal parameter to an actor.  If the actor is set up to respond to a signal by that name it will do so, otherwise it will have no effect.  Note: This is a relatively advanced actor message, as the actor you are sending the signal to must be pre-configured to respond to a signal message with the same name.
grammar: Signal signal

title: Set Scale
description: Constructs and returns a SetScale actor message based on the parameters.  This message sets the X,Y,Z scale of an actor*“s model, relative to the model”*s data entry X,Y,Z scale value, to the X,Y,Z parameter values.  This will take place over a number of game time seconds determined by the Blend Duration parameter.  If Blend Duration is left at 0, the change in scale will occur immediately.  Note: This only works on actors with models.
grammar: SetScale x, y, z blendDuration
prototype: string libNtve_gf_SetScale(fixed lp_x,fixed lp_y,fixed lp_z,fixed lp_blendDuration,);

title: Sound Set Muted
description: Constructs and returns a SoundSetMuted actor message based on the parameters.  This message will mute or unmute a sound depending on the Muted State parameter: 1 is muted, 0 is unmuted.  It can mute or unmute with or without a fade depending on the Fade parameter: 1 is fade, 0 is do not fade.
grammar: SoundSetMuted mutedState fade

title: Set Team Color
description: Constructs and returns a SetTeamColor actor message based on the parameters.  The Diffuse R,G,B parameter values determine the color of any diffuse textures on the model, such as the areas that display the color associated with their controlling player.  The Emissive R,G,B parameter values determine the color of any emissive textures on the model, such as the energy blades on the Zealot model.  This will only work on actor’s with a model.
grammar: SetTeamColor diffuseColor emissiveColor

title: Set Scale Absolute
description: Constructs and returns a SetScaleAbsolute actor message based on the parameters.  This message sets the absolute X,Y,Z scale of an actor’s model to the X,Y,Z parameter values.  This will take place over a number of game time seconds determined by the Blend Duration parameter.  If Blend Duration is left at 0, the change in scale will occur immediately.  Note: This only works on actors with models.
grammar: SetScaleAbsolute x, y, z blendDuration
prototype: string libNtve_gf_SetScaleAbsolute(fixed lp_x,fixed lp_y,fixed lp_z,fixed lp_blendDuration,);

title: Set Visibility
description: Constructs and returns a SetVisibility actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set the visibility state for the actor*“s model on or off, depending on the visibility parameter value.  If set to 0, the model will not show up.  If set to 1, the model will show up.  Other factors (such as if the model is under the fog of war) can affect an actor”*s visibility – this message cannot force an actor to be visibile if the game engine is causing it to be invisible.  The reverse is possible, however – this message can force an actor to be invisible.  In other words, invisibility always wins.
grammar: SetVisibility visible|Visibility

title: Sound Set Offset
description: Constructs and returns a SoundSetOffset actor message based on the parameters.  This message will offset the sound playback, in milliseconds, by a value equal to the Offset parameter.  This can be used to skip a portion of a sound played by a sound actor.  It will only work on sound actors.
grammar: SoundSetOffset offset

title: Sound Set Paused
description: Constructs and returns a SoundSetPaused actor message based on the parameters.  This message will paused or unpause a sound depending on the Paused State parameter: 1 is paused, 0 is unpaused.  It can pause or unpause with or without fade depending on the Fade parameter: 1 is fade, 0 is do not fade.
grammar: SoundSetPaused pausedState fade

title: Set Walk Anim Move Speed
description: Constructs and returns a SetWalkAnimMoveSpeed actor message.  This message alters an actor’s relative walking animation speed, 1=normal, less is faster, more is slower.
grammar: SetWalkAnimMoveSpeed value
raw_text: Set Walk Anim Move Speed—string<actormsg>— libNtve_gf_SetWalkAnimMoveSpeed

title: Set Rotation
description: Constructs and returns a SetRotation actor message based on the parameters.  This message will set the 3D rotation of the actor according to the Forward X,Y,Z parameters and the Up X,Y,Z parameters.
grammar: SetRotation forwardX forwardY __forwardZ|Forward Z __ upX upY upZ
prototype: string libNtve_gf_SetRotation(fixed lp_forwardX,fixed lp_forwardY,fixed lp_forwardZ,fixed lp_upX,fixed lp_upY,fixed lp_upZ,);

title: Texture Group Apply
description: Constructs and returns a TextureGroupApply message based on the parameters.  This message will change which set of textures are being displayed on an actor.  An example of this is the Vespene Geyser unit, which has a different texture set (or prop) for each tileset.  Note: This is a relatively advanced actor concept, as the textures must be set up in the data in order for a model to display them.
grammar: TextureGroupApply textureProps

title: Texture Group Remove
description: Constructs and returns a TextureGroupRemove actor message based on the parameters.  This message will remove a set of texture props associated with the name given in the Texture Props parameter.  The unit will revert to its default texture, if it is not already using it.
grammar: TextureGroupRemove textureProps

title: Texture Video Play
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoPlay actor message.  Plays a video texture in a texture slot with the specified rate and flags.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureVideoPlay texture fPS textureVideoPlayFlags soundType attachQuery
prototype: string libNtve_gf_MakeMsgTextureVideoPlay(string lp_texture,int lp_fPS,int lp_textureVideoPlayFlags,int lp_soundType,string lp_attachQuery,);

title: Status Decrement
description: Constructs and returns a StatusDecrement actor message.  This message is used to decrement an existing status variable, specified by the Status Variable parameter, on an actor.  If the status variable count is 0 a StatusOff message tied to that status variable is sent to the actor, which would respond in a pre-determined fashion to no longer having that status.  Note: This is a relatively advanced actor concept, as it requires an actor be setup to respond to a StatusOn or StatusOff message to have any effect.
grammar: StatusDecrement statusVariable

title: Status Increment
description: Constructs and returns a StatusIncrement actor message.  This message is used to increment an existing status variable, specified by the Status Variable parameter, on an actor.  If the status variable count is raised above 0 a StatusOn message tied to that status variable is sent to the actor, which would respond in a pre-determined fashion to having that status.  Note: This is a relatively advanced actor concept, as it requires an actor be setup to respond to a StatusOn or StatusOff message to have any effect.
grammar: StatusIncrement statusVariable

title: Texture Select By Match
description: Constructs and returns a TextureSelectByMatch actor message.  Selects a texture into the specified slot so as to best match the contents of the source slot.  Useful for populating Normal, Specular and Emissive slot components from the diffuse component.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureSelectByMatch slotName.slotComponent sourceSlotName.sourceSlotComponent
prototype: native string MakeMsgTextureSelectByMatch(string slotName,int slotComponent,string sourceSlotName,int sourceSlotComponent,);

title: Texture Video Play (Advanced)
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoPlay actor message.  Plays a video texture in a texture slot with the specified rate and flags.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureVideoPlay slotName.slotComponent fPS textureVideoPlayFlags soundType attachQuery
prototype: native string MakeMsgTextureVideoPlay(string slotName,int slotComponent,int fps,int textureVideoPlayFlags,int videoSoundType,string attachQuery,);

title: Texture Dump DB
description: Constructs and returns a TextureDumpDB actor message.  This message prints out all of the possible textures that could be applied to the model of an actor to the debug log.  It only works on actors with models.
grammar: TextureDumpDB

title: Actor Msg 4
description: Constructs and returns an actor message with four parameters. The actor message returned is formed by combining the Msg Name, Param 1, Param 2, Param 3 and Param 4 string parameters. This function is primarily used by other“Actor”type functions to assemble actor messages, although it can be used to assemble any actor message from scratch with the right know-how.
prototype: string libNtve_gf_ActorMsg4(string lp_msgName,string lp_param1,string lp_param2,string lp_param3,string lp_param4,);

title: Texture Dump
description: Constructs and returns a TextureDump actor message.  Prints the current texture list for an actor.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureDump

title: Texture Select By Slot
description: Constructs and returns a TextureSelectBySlot actor message.  Select a texture into a texture slot based on the specified texture expression.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureSelectBySlot slotName.slotComponent textureExpression
prototype: native string MakeMsgTextureSelectBySlot(string slotName,int slotComponent,string textureExpression,);

title: Texture Video Set Paused (Advanced)
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoSetPaused actor message.  Sets the pause state of the video texture in the specified texture slot.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureVideoSetPaused slotName.slotComponent pauseState
prototype: native string MakeMsgTextureVideoSetPaused(string slotName,int slotComponent,bool isPaused,);

title: Texture Select By ID
description: Constructs and returns a TextureSelectById actor message.  Selects a texture that is defined in game data.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureSelectById texture

title: Texture Video Set Paused
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoSetPaused actor message.  Sets the pause state of the video texture in the specified texture slot.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureVideoSetPaused texture pauseState

title: Texture Video Set Time
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoSetTime actor message.  Sets the play position (in seconds) of the video texture in the specified texture slot.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureVideoSetTime texture time

title: Texture Video Stop (Advanced)
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoStop actor message.  Stops a video texture in the specified texture slot.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureVideoStop slotName.slotComponent

title: Texture Video Set Time (Advanced)
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoSetTime actor message.  Sets the play position (in seconds) of the video texture in the specified texture slot.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureVideoSetTime slotName.slotComponent time
prototype: native string MakeMsgTextureVideoSetTime(string slotName,int slotComponent,fixed time,);

title: Texture Video Set Frame (Advanced)
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoSetFrame actor message.  Sets the frame of a video texture that is playing on the specified slot.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureVideoSetFrame slotName.slotComponent frame
prototype: native string MakeMsgTextureVideoSetFrame(string slotName,int slotComponent,int frame,);

title: Texture Video Stop All
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoStopAll actor message.  Stops all video textures on the actor it is sent to.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureVideoStopAll

title: Transition
description: Constructs and returns a Transition actor message.  Signals a generic transition“in”or“out”over a duration.  Works on rocker site actors.
grammar: Transition transitionType durationBase durationRange
prototype: native string MakeMsgTransition(int transitionType,fixed durationBase,fixed durationRange,);

title: Timer Kill
description: Constructs and returns a TimerKill message based on the parameters.  This message will remove a timer currently ticking on an actor.  It will not trigger its expiration effect.
grammar: TimerKill timerName

title: Texture Video Set Frame
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoSetFrame actor message.  Sets the frame of a video texture that is playing on the specified slot.  Works on actors with a model.
grammar: TextureVideoSetFrame texture frame

title: Kill Actor Scope
description: Kills an actor scope along with all of the actors in that scope.  An actor scope is a container for a set of actors.
grammar: Kill actor scope actorScope

title: Timer Set
description: Constructs and returns a TimerSet message based on the parameters.  This message will create a timer on the actor that will last for the Duration parameter in game time seconds.  The Timer Name parameter determines the name of the timer. When the timer expires a TimerExpired actor message is sent to the target actor, who would behave in a pre-determined fashion.  Note: This is a relatively advanced actor concept, as it requires an actor to be setup to respond to a TimerExpired actor message to have any effect.
grammar: TimerSet duration timerName

title: Kill Model
description: Tells a model actor type to play its death animation and then destroy itself.  It will only work on model actors, such as those created by the“Attach Model to Unit”action.
grammar: Kill actor model model

title: Kill All Particles
description: This will destroy all of the active particles on the map for all players.  Models that emit particles will still continue to do so, this will just kill the existing particles.
grammar: Kill all particles

title: Texture Video Stop
description: Constructs and returns a TextureVideoStop actor message.  Stops a video texture in the specified texture slot.  Works on actors with a model.

title: Remove Doodads in Region

title: Tactical AI - Cast
description: Suggests an order to a unit.  Works the same as Issue AI Order, but with additional parameters.  If the computer that controls the unit has the ability to execute the order (with respect to its current APM constraint), then it will do so.  The order will eventually be dropped if the computer is unable to execute it for a few seconds after it was issued.  If you need a unit to do something immediately, use Issue Order instead.  The Marker parameter is optional, pass in null if you don’t want to use it.
grammar: Suggest order order for unit unit with retreat toggle of retreat. Optionally passes marker marker info
prototype: native int AICast(unit u, order o, marker m, bool retreat);

title: Tactical AI Filter Life Per Marker
description: Sets the specified AI Filter to only include units whose current life is greater than the number of specified markers currently on them times the value specified in the Each parameter.  In this context, markers are an AI system construct used to mark targets of abilities before the ability’s effects execute. title: Tactical AI Filter Life Modifier
description: Sets the specified AI Filter to modify the way it calculates life values for units with the specified attribute.  For example, if the attribute was set to Armored, and the Amount set to 100, then the filter would consider Armored units to have 100 more life than they actually have.  This can only be set once per AI Filter, so calling this action multiple times with the same AI Filter will simply override previous calls.  Use this and the other“Tactical AI Filter”actions to set up an AI Filter, which can then be used in a“Tactical AI Filter - Run”function to filter a unit group.

title: Turn Animation Properties On With Blend In/Out
description: Enable a special animation property, play the Blend In Animation when the property is turned on, and play the Blend Out Animation when the property is turned off. The unit must be set up to use the specified animation property. An example of an animation property is“Fly”for Terran production buildings. If the unit is not set up to use one of the Blend Animations, then it will ignore it. Use the Turn Animation Properties Off action to turn off the property that was turned on by this action.

title: Query Animation Length By Identifier
description: Starts a query for the animation length and animation time remaining of the specific animation name for the given actor.  Note that the animation length and remaining time may vary from player to player due to different game states or localities.  This function will query the maximum length and maximum remaining time of all players when there are different values.  Note that you may only do 512 animation length queries maximum in a single game.  Use the“Last Animation Query”function to get a handle to this query which can be used to get the information retrieved after the query is complete.

prototype: void libNtve_gf_PlayAnimationOnDoodadsInRegion(region lp_target,string lp_doodadType,string lp_identifier,string lp_animation,int lp_flags,fixed lp_blendTime,);
raw_text: Play Animation On Doodads In Region—void— libNtve_gf_PlayAnimationOnDoodadsInRegion

title: Wait for Animation
description: Waits until an animation is a specified amount of seconds from the offset type.   Note that the animations and animation times may vary from player to player due to different game states or localities so this waits for all players to report that they have reached the specified point.
grammar: Wait until identifier playback is offset seconds from the offsetType of the animation for actor actor
prototype: native void AnimWait(actor a,string animName,fixed offset,int offsetType,);

title: Clear Animation
description: Clears an animation with the chosen name (Identifier) on an actor.
grammar: Clear the identifier animation for target

title: Play Animation
description: Plays an animation on an actor with a chosen name (Identifier).  The Identifier is used to clear the animation or otherwise modify it after it starts playing.
grammar: Play animation animation for target as identifier, using flags|Options options and blendTime blend time
prototype: void libNtve_gf_PlayAnimation(actor lp_target,string lp_identifier,string lp_animation,int lp_flags,fixed lp_blendTime,);

title: Query Animation Length By Props
description: Starts a query for the animation length of the specific animation props for the given actor.  Note that the animation length time may vary from player to player due to different game states or localities.  This function will query the maximum length time of all players when there are different values.  Note that you may only do 512 animation length queries maximum in a single game.  Use the“Last Animation Query”function to get a handle to this query which can be used to get the information retrieved after the query is complete.
grammar: Query the length of animation defined by props animProps for actor
raw_text: Query Animation Length By Props—void— AnimLengthQueryByProps

title: Wait for Animation Length Queries
description: Waits until all queries sent with“Query Animation Length”functions have been received for all players.
grammar: Wait until all active animation length queries are finished
raw_text: Wait for Animation Length Queries—void— AnimLengthQueryWait

title: Animation Length
description: Returns the animation length previously queried.  Pass this function the handle returned by the“Last Animation Query”to get the value, but make sure that you call Wait for Animation Length Queries first to ensure that the animation query is finished.
grammar: The length returned by animation query handle

title: Set Animation Time
description: Skips ahead in an animation, chosen by the Identifier parameter, to a time chosen by the Time value. If the Scaled parameter is set to Absolute, the time will be set according to the actual length of the animation for the model. If the Scaled parameter is set to Scaled, the time will be set relevent to the animation length determined by the actor controlling the animation.
grammar: Set the identifier scaled|Absolute/Scaled animation time for target to time
prototype: void libNtve_gf_SetAnimationTime(actor lp_target,string lp_identifier,fixed lp_time,bool lp_scaled,);

title: Set Animation Time Scale
description: Sets the length of an animation, chosen by the Identifier parameter, to the Scale parameter relevant to the actual length of the animation for the model.
grammar: Set the identifier animation time scale for target to scale

title: Set Animation Duration
description: Set the amount of seconds to play a specified animation.
grammar: Set the identifier animation duration for target to duration seconds

title: Set Animation Completion
description: Sets the progress completion percent for an animation.  Does the same thing as“Set Animation Time”, but with different parameters.
grammar: Set the identifier animation completion for target to percent percent

title: Load Model Animation
description: Loads a specified animation for a model.  Note: This is a specialized action that generally relates to storymode models.  It allows a model to load an additional set of animations set up for it.  If a model is not set up to have additional animations, this action will not work.
grammar: Load animation animation for model model

values.name: Default
values.code: "Default"

title: Animation Time Remaining
description: Returns the animation time remaining previously queried.  Pass this function the handle returned by the“Last Animation Query”functions to get the value, but make sure that you call Wait for Animation first to ensure that the animation query is finished.  This function is only valid with a handle created by“Query Animation Length By Name”.
grammar: The timer remaining returned by animation query handle

title: Load Overriding Model Animation
description: Loads a specified animation for a model, and invalidates any existing duplicate sequences that already exist in the model.  Note: This is a specialized action that generally relates to storymode models.  It allows a model to load an additional set of animations set up for it.  If a model is not set up to have additional animations, this action will not work.
grammar: Load animation animation for model model

title: Unload Model Animation
description: Loads a specified animation for a model.  Note: This is a specialized action that generally relates to storymode models.  It will remove additional animation sets that have been loaded to the model through triggers.
grammar: Unload animation animation for model model

title: Load Animation for Unit Type
description: Loads an animation for a unit.  Note: This is a specialized action that generally relates to storymode models.  It allows a model to load an additional set of animations set up for it.  If a model is not set up to have additional animations, this action will not work.
grammar: Load animation animation for unit type unitType

description: Loads an animation for a unit, and invalidates any existing duplicate sequences that already exist.  Note: This is a specialized action that generally relates to storymode models.  It allows a model to load an additional set of animations set up for it.  If a model is not set up to have additional animations, this action will not work.
grammar: Load animation animation for unit type unitType

title: Open Bank
description: Opens a bank with the name specified by the Name parameter, for the chosen Player. If a bank with the specified name does not already exist, one will be created for the chosen player. A bank is an xml file where you can store information for later use, even across maps. It’s a good method of saving information to pass between maps in a custom campaign or multiple part custom map. See the“Store Boolean”action for further information.
grammar: Open bank name for player player
prototype: native bank BankLoad(string name, int player);

title: Save Bank
description: Saves the specified bank file, allowing you to access stored values later. If a bank is not saved, any changes made to it will be lost when the mission ends.
grammar: Save bank bank

title: Reload Bank
description: Reloads the specified bank file, allowing you to access stored values later. If a bank has unsaved changes they will be lost when it reloads.
grammar: Reload bank bank

title: Last Opened Bank
description: Returns the last bank that was opened.
grammar: Last opened bank

title: Wait For Bank
description: Waits for the specified bank file to finish reloading.
grammar: Wait for bank bank

title: Store Real
description: Stores a Real value to the specified Bank. The Section and Key parameters determine where the value is stored in the bank file, and must be used to load the value later. You must save a bank after storing a value, otherwise the value will not exist to load later.
grammar: Store real value as key of section section in bank bank
prototype: native void BankValueSetFromFixed(bank b,string section,string key,fixed value,);

title: Store Boolean

grammar: Store boolean value as key of section section in bank bank
prototype: native void BankValueSetFromFlag(bank b,string section,string key,bool value,);

prototype: native void BankValueSetFromPoint(bank b,string section,string key,point value,);

prototype: native void BankValueSetFromText(bank b,string section,string key,text value,);

title: Store Integer

title: Store String

prototype: native void BankValueSetFromString(bank b,string section,string key,string value,);

title: Store Unit
description: Stores a Unit in the specified Bank. The Section and Key parameters determine where the Unit is stored in the bank file, and must be used to restore the unit later. You must save a bank after storing a value, otherwise the value will not exist to load later. Unit values are recalled with the“Restore Unit”action.

title: MakeEmptyBitMask
description: Makes a completely zeroed out BitMask
grammar: Make Empty BitMask

title: BitMaskReset
description: Resets the BitMask
grammar: Clear bitMask|BitMask

title: BitMaskFalseIndex
description: Returns true if the index is set to 0 in the bit mask.
grammar: Is indexToCheck|Bit in bitMask|BitMask not toggled
prototype: native bool BitMaskFalseIndex(bitmask mask, int index);

title: BitMaskTrueIndex
description: Returns true if the index is set to 1 in the bit mask.
grammar: Is indexToCheck|Bit in bitMask|BitMask toggled
prototype: native bool BitMaskTrueIndex(bitmask mask, int index);

title: BitMaskSetIndex
description: Sets a flag on the bit mask either on or off depending on the boolean Set On. Returns true if the setting worked.
prototype: native void BitMaskSetIndex(bitmask mask,int index,bool on,);

title: BitMaskCountOnBits
description: Counts all the ones in the bit mask.
grammar: Count of toggled Bits in bitMask|BitMask

title: BitMaskInvert
description: Applies a negation to all the bits in the BitMask (BitMask = !BitMask)

title: BitMaskAddBitMask
description: Returns —void
grammar: Add bitMaskToAdd|BitMask to receivingBitMask|BitMask
prototype: native void BitMaskAddBitMask(bitmask left, bitmask right);

title: BitMaskIsEqual
description: Compares two bit masks for total equality on all flags.
grammar: lHS|Left BitMask equals rHS|Right BitMask
prototype: native bool BitMaskIsEqual(bitmask lhs, bitmask rhs);

title: BitMaskAndBitMask
description: Ands the two BitMasks together placing the result in the Receiving BitMask (LHS = LHS & RHS)
prototype: native void BitMaskAndBitMask(bitmask lhs, bitmask rhs);

title: BitMaskLeftShift
description: Left shifts the receiving BitMask by count (receiving << count)
prototype: native void BitMaskLeftShift(bitmask mask, int count);

title: BitMaskRightShift
description: Right shifts the receiving BitMask by count (receiving >> count)
prototype: native void BitMaskRightShift(bitmask mask, int count);

title: Make Camera Look At
description: Adjusts the camera to look at the new target point without changing the camera eye position.
grammar: Make the camera look at p for player player over duration seconds with initialVelocity% initial velocity, decelerate% deceleration
prototype: native void CameraLookAt(int player,point p,fixed duration,fixed velocity,fixed decelerate,);

title: Camera Yaw of Player
description: Returns the current camera yaw of a player.  Camera yaw is tracked for all players in a multiplayer game, but the queried locations will be slightly old due to network latency.
grammar: Current camera yaw of player player

title: Camera Distance of Player
description: Returns the current camera distance of a player.  Camera distance is tracked for all players in a multiplayer game, but the queried locations will be slightly old due to network latency.
grammar: Current camera distance of player player

title: Camera Pitch of Player
description: Returns the current camera pitch of a player.  Camera pitch is tracked for all players in a multiplayer game, but the queried locations will be slightly old due to network latency.
grammar: Current camera pitch of player player

title: Save Camera
description: Saves the current camera configurations.  You can recall the camera settings using“Restore Camera”.
grammar: Save the current camera settings for player player

title: Camera Target Of Player
description: Returns the current camera target of a player.  Camera targets are tracked for all players in a multiplayer game, but the queried locations will be slightly old due to network latency.
grammar: Current camera target of player player

title: BitMaskOrBitMask
description: Ors the two BitMasks together placing the result in the Receiving BitMask (LHS = LHS | RHS)
prototype: native void BitMaskOrBitMask(bitmask lhs, bitmask rhs);

title: Pan Camera
description: Pans the camera to the center of a region or point.  If smart panning is enabled, the camera will not pan if it is already looking at the target point.
grammar: Pan the camera for player player to p over duration seconds with initialVelocity% initial velocity, decelerate% deceleration, and smart|Do/Do Not use smart panning
prototype: native void CameraPan(int player,point p,fixed duration,fixed velocity,fixed decelerate,bool smart,);

title: BitMaskXorBitMask
description: Xors the two BitMasks together placing the result in the Receiving BitMask (LHS = LHS ^ RHS)
prototype: native void BitMaskXorBitMask(bitmask lhs, bitmask rhs);

title: Set Camera Channel On Portrait
description: In order to use this, you must first create a portrait through triggers.  You can then use this action to have a camera object on that portrait render to a render-to-texture channel.

title: Set Camera Bounds
description: Sets the camera bounds within a region. This will limit the specified Players from moving their camera beyond the specified Bounds. title: Cinematic Mode
description: Turns on/off cinematic mode for a player(s). Cinematic mode hides the normal gameplay UI.  When cinematic mode is turned on, the state of the gameplay UI is cached, and when cinematic mode is turned off, the state of the gameplay UI is restored from that cache.  Therefore, changing visibility settings of gameplay UI elements while cinematic mode is turned on will have no effect.  This mode will also mute certain sound channels.
grammar: Turn cinematic mode onOff for players over duration seconds

title: Set Cinematic Transition Style
description: Changes the behavior when transitioning to/from cinematic mode.  When set to Story, transmissions will not be stopped automatically.
grammar: Set the transition style of cinematic mode to style

title: Global Cinematic Setting (Fixed Seed On/Off)
description: Turns on/off the aspects of cinematic mode that are not player-specific, such as game speed.  This will also turn on fixed random seed, so random rolls done while in cinematic mode will come out the same every time it is run.  These settings affect all players, so this action should not be used if some players in a game are viewing a cinematic while others are not.  This action is intended to be used in conjunction with the“Cinematic Mode”action.
grammar: Turn global cinematic setting onOff

title: Global Cinematic Setting
description: Turns on/off the aspects of cinematic mode that are not player-specific, such as game speed. These settings affect all players, so this action should not be used if some players in a game are viewing a cinematic while others are not.  This action is intended to be used in conjunction with the“Cinematic Mode”action.
grammar: Turn global cinematic setting onOff

title: Movie Function
description: This event is only usable if you play a movie texture on a model, and that movie texture has a .event file with the same name as the movie texture. title: Current DateTime
description: Returns the best guess of the current time for players in the real world as a server-timezone adjusted DateTime.

title: Initial DateTime
description: Returns the best guess of when the game started for players in the real world as a server-timezone adjusted DateTime. Returns the time the game started loading if called during loading screen (initialization) and the time gameplay started afterwards.
grammar: GameStartDateTime()

title: Get DateTime Day
description: Get the day in the current month of a DateTime. (1 is the first day of the month)
grammar: dateTime Day

title: Get DateTime Month
description: Get the month in the current year of a DateTime. (1 is January)
grammar: dateTime Month

title: Get DateTime Year
description: Get the year of a DateTime.
grammar: dateTime Year

title: Get DateTime Minute
description: Get the minute in the current hour of a DateTime.
grammar: dateTime Minute

title: Get DateTime Hour
description: Get the hour in the current day of a DateTime. (0 is 12 AM)
grammar: dateTime Hour

title: Get DateTime Second
description: Get the second  in the current minute of a DateTime.
grammar: dateTime Second

title: Get DateTime Weekday Index
description: Returns the index of the weekday that the given DateTime has. (0 is Sunday.)
grammar: datetime weekday index

title: Synchronous Game Start Time
description: Returns the best guess of when the game started for players in the real world as a server-timezone adjusted UNIX epoch (seconds since January 1, 1970). Returns the time the game started loading if called on the loading screen and the time gameplay started later.
grammar: Synchronous Game Start Time

title: Current Synchronous Game Time
description: Returns the best guess of the current time for players in the real world as a server-timezone adjusted UNIX epoch (seconds since January 1, 1970).
grammar: Current Synchronous Game Time

title: Get Current Day of Week

grammar: Current Weekday()

description: Returns the day of the week of a DateTime.

grammar: datetime month

title: DateTime is Weekend
description: Returns true if a DateTime is on a Saturday or Sunday.

title: Effect History Of Unit
description: Returns an Effect History object that can be used to inspect the history of effects for a unit.  In order for a unit to register effects to its history, the unit must specify an Effect History Limit value, and any effects you want to track must specify a Caster History value.  The Caster History value specifies the category under which you want to track the effect.  You can use Effect History Entry Type to query the category for an Effect History entry.  A max count of 0 (the default) will get all available entries.
grammar: Effect History of Unit unit with maxCount entries.

title: Effect History Entry Amount
description: Returns —fixed
grammar: Amount amount of effect history history entry index. total|Do/Do Not use total.
prototype: native fixed EffectHistoryGetAmountFixed(effecthistory inHistory,int inIndex,int inAmount,bool inTotal,);

title: Effect History Entry Amount As Integer
description: Returns —int
grammar: Amount amount of effect history history entry index. total|Do/Do Not use total.
prototype: native int EffectHistoryGetAmountInt(effecthistory inHistory,int inIndex,int inAmount,bool inTotal,);
raw_text: Effect History Entry Amount As Integer—int— EffectHistoryGetAmountInt

title: Effect History Count
description: Returns the number of entries in the specified Effect History.  Use Effect History Of Unit to get an Effect History.
grammar: Count of Effect History history

title: Effect History Entry Effect
description: If the Effect parameter is set to Root, this will return the effect that was at the root of the effect tree for the effect that was logged in the Effect History at the specified Index.  If the Effect parameter is set to Current, then this will return the effect that was logged in the Effect History at the specified Index.
grammar: Effect effect for effect history history entry index
prototype: native string EffectHistoryGetEffect(effecthistory inHistory,int inIndex,int inEffect,);

title: Set Creep Speed
description: Sets the speed of creep.  Growth determines how rapidly creep will expand from its origin point.  Decay determines how rapidly creep will receed if there are no creep generators nearby.  Blend determines how rapidly the creep will visually appear, and is seperate from the functional purposes of creep.

prototype: native void CreepSetSpeed(int inType, fixed inPercent);

title: Chat Message
description: This event fires when the specified chat message is typed by the specified player.  If exact match is selected, then the string the player enters must be identical to the specified string for the event to fire.  If partial match is selected, then the event will fire if the player enters the specified string plus any number of additional characters after it.  Use the“Chat String”function to refer to the message being entered.  Use“Triggering Player”to get the player that typed the message.

title: Online Map To Map Load
description: Executes an online map to map load. Ends the game for all observers and players that have not already won, tied, lost or left the game, then transitions them together into another map published by the same author. The destination map is chosen by a map slot. Map slots must be set using the managed published dialog. Ensure that the source and destination maps have the same set of user controlled players to prevent map transition failures. Default variants and game settings will be used to create the destination map. Only supported for online games.
grammar: Map to map load into author map slot mapSlot|slot. victoryPlayers|Players win. defeatPlayers|Players lose.
prototype: native void OnlineMapToMapLoad(int mapSlot,playergroup victoryPlayers,playergroup defeatPlayers,);
raw_text: Online Map To Map Load—void— OnlineMapToMapLoad

title: Wait
description: Pauses a trigger for a specified amount of time.  Real time is normal time.  Game time can pass faster or slower depending on the game speed.  AI time passes at the same rate as game time, but can be paused or unpaused while real time and game time are still running.  It is useful to pause the AI time while a player is watching a cinematic, so timed attack waves will wait until the player is finished with the cinematic before resuming their countdowns.
grammar: Wait t timeType|Real/Game/AI Time seconds
prototype: native void Wait(fixed inSecs, int inTimeType);

description: Sets the alliance settings of one player towards another player, but not vice-versa.  This action will determine how the Source Player treats the Target Player, such as whether or not the Source Player*“s units fight the Target Player"s units.  Pushable means that the Target Player"s units are allowed to push the Source Player”*s units out of the way if they are trying to path through them.

description: Returns a point along the line defined by the two given points. The position along the line is determined by the given fraction. 0.0 means the source point, 1.0 means the target point, and 0.5 means half-way between the two. Values outside the 0.0 to 1.0 range result in valid extrapolations along the line beyond the given points.

title: Enable/Disable Tech Tree Restrictions
description: Enables or disables all tech restrictions for the specified player.  When tech restrictions are enabled, then all ability commands that are flagged as Restricted or Suppressed will not show up on unit command cards in game.  Use“Allow/Disallow Ability For Player”,“Allow/Disallow Behavior For Player”,“Allow/Disallow Unit For Player”, and“Allow/Disallow Upgrade For Player”to enable Restricted or Suppressed commands.  If a command is Restricted, then the ability, behavior, unit, or upgrade that is tied to it must be directly allowed.  If a command is Suppressed, however, then it may be enabled when something else is allowed.  For example, if the train Banshee command is Restricted, and the build Factory and build Starport commands are Suppressed, allowing the Banshee will also allow the Factory and Starport, because you cannot train a Banshee without first constructing those buildings.
grammar: isEnabled|Enable/Disable tech tree restrictions for player p

title: Run Trigger By Name
description: Executes the specified trigger.  If Trigger A calls“Run Trigger”to execute Trigger B, then Trigger A will not continue execution until either Trigger B returns, reaches the end of its actions, or reaches a“Wait”action.  If Trigger B has a“Wait”action, and the Wait parameter of“Run Trigger”was set to Don’t Wait, then Trigger A will resume execution.  If the Wait parameter was set to Wait, then Trigger A will not resume execution until Trigger B has completed or returned.
grammar: Run t  (check|Check/Ignore Conditions, wait|Wait/Don’t Wait until it finishes)

title: Current Trigger Thread SetFlags
description: This is only used to set flags on the current galaxy thread so that the internal execution can know what type of thread is being executed.

title: Turn Trigger On/Off
description: Turning a trigger off will prevent it from responding to events.  A turned off trigger can still be run directly via a“Run Trigger”action.
grammar: Turn t state|On/Off
prototype: native void TriggerEnable(trigger t, bool enable);

title: Run Trigger
description: Executes the specified trigger.  If Trigger A calls“Run Trigger”to execute Trigger B, then Trigger A will not continue execution until either Trigger B returns, reaches the end of its actions, or reaches a“Wait”action.  If Trigger B has a“Wait”action, and the Wait parameter of“Run Trigger”was set to Don’t Wait, then Trigger A will resume execution.  If the Wait parameter was set to Wait, then Trigger A will not resume execution until Trigger B has completed or returned.
grammar: Run t  (check|Check/Ignore Conditions, wait|Wait/Don’t Wait until it finishes)
prototype: native void TriggerExecute(trigger t,bool testConds,bool waitUntilDone,);

title: Generic Event Parameter

title: Evaluate Trigger Conditions
description: Tests a trigger*“s conditions and returns true if they passed.  This does not actually run the actions of the trigger.  Note that the trigger”*s event is not actually firing, so functions that normally respond to events, such as“Triggering Unit”, will not be meaningful in the context of using this function to testing a trigger’s conditions.
grammar: Evaluate t conditions

title: Hotkey Pressed
description: Returns the hotkey pressed.  Use in response to a“Hotkey Pressed”event.
grammar: Hotkey Pressed

title: Mouse Wheel
description: Only register for this event when you absolutely need it, as it will generate network traffic from all players for every mouse wheel move they make.  This event fires when a player moves the mouse wheel.  Use“Mouse Wheel Spin”to get the amount the mouse wheel moved.
grammar: Player player moves mouse wheel.

title: Mouse Moved
description: Only register for this event when you absolutely need it, as it will generate network traffic from all players for every mouse move they make.  This event fires when a player moves themouse.  Use“Mouse Moved UI Pos X”and“Mouse Moved UI Pos Y”to get the UI X,Y position where the player moved the mouse.  Use“Mouse Moved World Pos X”,“Mouse Moved World Pos Y”, and“Mouse Moved World Pos Z”to get the X,Y,Z world position where the player moved the mouse.
grammar: Player player moves mouse.

title: Hide Cinematic Text
description: Hides all cinematic text that is currently displaying for the specified players.
grammar: Hide cinematic text for players

title: Hotkey Pressed
description: Only register for this event when you absolutely need it, as it will generate network traffic from all players for every hotkey they press.  This event fires when a player presses the specified hotkey. Use“HotKey Pressed”to get the hotkey that was pressed.WARNING: If code handles the hotkey press (for example, Army Select is pressed and code responds by selecting all army units), this event WILL NOT fire!
grammar: Player player presses hotkey key down|up/down
prototype: native void TriggerAddEventHotkeyPressed(trigger t,int player,int hotkey,bool down,);

title: Key Pressed
description: Only register for this event when you absolutely need it, as it will generate network traffic from all players for every button they press.  This event fires when a player presses the specified key.  Use“Key Pressed”to get the key that was pressed.  Use“Alt Key Pressed”,“Control Key Pressed”, and“Shift Key Pressed”to find out what modifier keys were pressed.
grammar: Player player presses key key down|up/down with shift s2|Enabled, control c2|Enabled, alt a2|Enabled
prototype: native void TriggerAddEventKeyPressed(trigger t,int player,int key,bool down,int s,int c,int a,);

title: Button Pressed
description: Returns the button pressed.  Use in response to a“Button Pressed”event.
grammar: Button Pressed

title: Mouse Clicked
description: Only register for this event when you absolutely need it, as it will generate network traffic from all players for every mouse click they make.  This event fires when a player clicks a mouse button.  Use“Mouse Clicked Button”to get the button that was clicked.  Use“Mouse Clicked UI Pos X”and“Mouse Clicked UI Pos Y”to get the UI X,Y position where the player clicked the mouse.  Use“Mouse Clicked World Pos X”,“Mouse Clicked World Pos Y”, and“Mouse Clicked World Pos Z”to get the X,Y,Z world position where the player clicked the mouse.  Use“Alt Key Pressed”,“Control Key Pressed”, and“Shift Key Pressed”to find out what modifier keys were pressed.
grammar: Player player clicks button|Button mouse button down|up/down.
prototype: native void TriggerAddEventMouseClicked(trigger t,int player,int mouseButton,bool down,);

title: Unit Dies
description: This event fires when a unit dies.  Use“Damage Source Position”to get the position of the unit that dealt the killing blow.  Use“Killing Player”to get the owner of the unit that dealt the killing blow.  Use“Killing Unit”to get the unit that dealt the killing blow.  Use“Triggering Death Type Check”to get the death type.  Use“Triggering Player”to get the owner of the unit that died.  Use“Triggering Unit”to get the unit that died.  Use“Experience Level Of Unit”to get the experience level of the unit that died.  Use“Total Experience Of Unit”to get the total number of experience points the unit had before it died.
grammar: u dies
prototype: native void TriggerAddEventUnitDied(trigger t, unitref u);

title: Unit Absorbs Damage
description: This event fires when a unit absorbs damage due to a behavior damage response.  Use“Triggering Unit”to get the unit that absorbed damage.  Use“Triggering Behavior”to get the behavior that absorbed damage on the unit.  Use“Triggering Damage Absorbed”to get the amount of damage absorbed.  Use“Damaging Unit”and“Damaging Player”to get the unit and player that caused the damage.  Use“Damaging Effect”to get the effect that caused the damage.
grammar: u absorbs damage with behavior behavior
prototype: native void TriggerAddEventUnitDamageAbsorbed(trigger inTrigger,unitref inUnit,string inBehavior,);

title: Create Revealer
description: Creates a revealer that covers the region specified, for the player specified.  Use the“Last Created Revealer”function to refer to this revealer.
grammar: Create a visibility revealer for player player within area
prototype: native revealer VisRevealerCreate(int player, region area);

title: Visibility Is Enabled
description: Returns true if the visibilty type for the game matches the type specified.
grammar: type|Visibility Type visibility is enabled

title: Set Fog of War Alpha
description: Override a player’s Fog of War transparency level. (0 = transparent, 100 = opaque)
prototype: native void VisSetFoWAlpha(int player, fixed alpha);
raw_text: Set Fog of War Alpha—void— VisSetFoWAlpha

title: Point Is Visible For Player
description: Returns true if the point is visible (by being within a unit’s line of sight or by being revealed) for the specified player.
grammar: point is visible for Player player
prototype: native bool VisIsVisibleForPlayer(int player, point inPos);
raw_text: Point Is Visible For Player—bool— VisIsVisibleForPlayer

title: Reset Fog of War Alpha
description: Reset a player’s Fog of War transparency level to the default value.
raw_text: Reset Fog of War Alpha—void— VisResetFoWAlpha

title: Last Created Revealer
description: Returns the last revealer created via a“Create Revealer”action.
grammar: Last created revealer

title: Enable/Disable Revealer
description: Enables/Disables the specified revealer but does not destroy it.
grammar: enable|Enable/Disable r
prototype: native void VisRevealerEnable(revealer r, bool enable);

title: Update Revealer
description: Updates the revealer after a region has been moved or changed.
grammar: Update r to match its region

title: Destroy Revealer
description: Destroys the specified revealer.
grammar: Destroy r

title: User Data (Text)
description: Returns a value from user data for the given type, instance, and field. User data is defined in the Data module.
grammar: User Data (userType, instance, field, index)
prototype: native text UserDataGetText(string inType,string inInstance,string inField,int inIndex,);
